title: The Compatibility Problem: Determinism and Libertarian Free Will
description: An analysis of the core tension between causal determinism and the libertarian conception of free will, a central problem in metaphysics since antiquity.
definition: The philosophical problem examining whether libertarian free will—the ability of an agent to choose otherwise given identical past circumstances—can be coherently reconciled with causal determinism—the thesis that every event, including human choices, is necessitated by prior events and the laws of nature. Incompatibilists argue they are mutually exclusive; compatibilists redefine free will to align with determinism.

proofs.description: Grounded in historical positions: Hume's compatibilism, Kant's transcendental idealism, contemporary work by Chalmers and Dennett.
The consequence argument (if determinism is true, our actions are the consequences of laws and events in the distant past, over which we have no control) presents a logical challenge to free will.
